circuits.designation: General receptacles — living + bedrooms + hallway (NEC 2023 Article 210.12(A) AFCI)
circuits.rcd_exception_citation: AFCI-only circuit per NEC 2023 Article 210.12(A); no GFCI required for general receptacles outside NEC 210.8 scope (US AFCI ≠ IEC §411.3.3 Type A RCD). US AFCI provides combination arc-fault protection per UL 1699 and is functionally distinct from IEC residual-current devices.

non_compliance_flags.message: Schema special_location enum lacks US-specific categories (garage, basement, laundry). Garage GFCI applies per NEC 2023 Article 210.8(A)(2); basement GFCI per 210.8(A)(5); laundry GFCI per 210.8(A)(10). Documented as info-level; bathroom mapped to bathroom_zone_3 and outdoor maps natively.

Schema rcd_posture enum is IEC-derived (Type A 30 mA per §411.3.3). US implementation uses GFCI (4-6 mA personnel-protection trip per UL 943) and AFCI (combination arc-fault per UL 1699). Pragmatic mapping: GFCI circuits → type_a_30ma_per_§411_3_3 with rcd_type=GFCI + rcd_sensitivity_ma=10 (smallest enum value, since 4-6 mA is not a schema option); C03 AFCI-only → no_rcd_with_documented_§411_exception with full citation. Schema gap noted for future runtime.

All receptacles in dwelling units must be listed tamper-resistant per NEC 2023 Article 406.12. All 31 receptacles in this design are NEMA 5-15 TR or NEMA 5-20 TR; outdoor receptacles are additionally listed weather-resistant per NEC 406.9(B) and installed in in-use covers.

Zs / loop-impedance verification deferred to calc.zs_loop_impedance per WI3. Per-circuit tool_call_pending_for_zs_verification=true; ground-fault loop impedance to be confirmed at install per NEC 2023 Article 250.4(A)(5) and NEC 110.10 (interrupting rating + impedance compatible with available fault current).

assumptions: Utility-declared Ze=0.25 Ω at the service entrance neutral-ground bond; not yet site-measured.
Utility-declared PSCC=10 kA at MAIN-PANEL busbar; all branch breakers selected with 10 kA AIC (interrupting rating) per NEC 2023 Article 110.9 + UL 489.

120/240 V split-phase nominal (center-tapped single-phase from utility transformer). Line-to-line=240 V, line-to-neutral=120 V. All 120 V branch circuits in this design draw between one ungrounded conductor and neutral.

System type TN-C-S in schema enum is the closest analogue to a US service: the service-entrance neutral is bonded to ground at the main panel (main bonding jumper per NEC 250.24(B)); downstream branch circuits use separate equipment grounding conductor (EGC) — functionally TN-S downstream of the main bonding jumper.

NM-B cable (Type NM-B, 12 AWG and 14 AWG with bare copper EGC) assumed for branch wiring per NEC 2023 Article 334. PVC_70 insulation in the schema encodes the THHN/THWN-equivalent 70 °C rating typical for 14/12 AWG NM-B.
Diversity per NEC 2023 Article 220.40-220.55 dwelling-unit calculation; small-appliance branches per 220.52(A) (1500 VA each, 2 minimum); laundry branch per 220.52(B) (1500 VA).
Outdoor receptacles installed in extra-duty in-use covers per NEC 406.9(B)(1) and rated IP66 in the schema (closest IP analogue to UL 514D + NEMA 3R weather-resistant rating).
All branch breakers AFCI / GFCI / dual-function devices listed and recognized under UL 489 (interrupting rating), UL 943 (GFCI), and UL 1699 (AFCI) as applicable.

Drafting follows AIA CAD Layer Guidelines 2007 (US AEC convention) for layered electrical drawings; sheet Arch_D at 1/4"=1' imperial scale is industry-standard for single-family residential floor plans.

drawing_notes: All receptacles NEMA 5-15 or NEMA 5-20 duplex tamper-resistant (TR) per NEC 2023 Article 406.12.

Kitchen countertop receptacles at 1100 mm AFF (~43" — ~6" above standard 36" countertop) split between C01 (3) and C02 (3); both circuits AFCI+GFCI dual-function breakers per NEC 210.8(A)(6) + 210.12(B).
General-purpose living/bedroom/hallway receptacles at 450 mm AFF (~18") on a 15A AFCI branch (C03) per NEC 210.12(A); no GFCI required outside NEC 210.8 scope.
Bathroom receptacle at 1100 mm AFF above counter on dedicated 20A GFCI branch (C04) per NEC 210.8(A)(1) + 210.11(C)(3). Tamper-resistant + GFCI receptacle.
Garage GFCI receptacles (3 × NEMA 5-20 TR) on C05 per NEC 210.8(A)(2); workshop / overhead-door wall locations at 1100 mm AFF.
Outdoor receptacles (2 × NEMA 5-20 TR-WR weather-resistant) at 1100 mm AFF on external wall on C06 per NEC 210.8(A)(3); extra-duty in-use covers per NEC 406.9(B)(1).
Basement GFCI receptacles (3 × NEMA 5-20 TR) on C07 per NEC 210.8(A)(5); 1100 mm AFF on wall.
Laundry dedicated branch (C08) per NEC 210.11(C)(2); 20A GFCI breaker per NEC 210.8(A)(10) (laundry area within 6 ft of laundry tubs / washing machines).

Drawing follows AIA CAD Layer Guidelines 2007 (US AEC convention): Discipline-MajorGroup-MinorGroup layer naming (e.g., E-POWR-RECP for receptacles, E-POWR-CIRC for circuits, E-POWR-PANL for panelboards).

rationale.chat_summary: US single-family residence (~160 m² / 1700 ft²) on NEC 2023. 120/240V split-phase TN-C-S supply, Ze=0.25Ω, PSCC=10kA. 8 branch circuits via MAIN-PANEL. C01+C02 kitchen 20A AFCI+GFCI dual per NEC 210.8(A)(6)+210.12(B); C03 general 15A AFCI per 210.12(A); C04-C07 GFCI 20A per 210.8 (bathroom/garage/outdoor/basement); C08 laundry 20A per 210.11(C)(2). All receptacles tamper-resistant per NEC 406.12. NEMA 5-15/5-20 duplex. calc.zs_loop_impedance pending.

sections.title: Jurisdiction + Supply
sections.summary: US jurisdiction, NEC 2023 (NFPA 70) primary code. 120/240 V split-phase TN-C-S service: center-tapped single-phase from utility transformer with main bonding jumper at MAIN-PANEL per NEC 250.24(B). Declared Ze=0.25 Ω and PSCC=10 kA at the service-entrance busbar. All branch breakers AIC ≥ 10 kA per NEC 110.9.

decisions.label: Split-phase service confirmed
decisions.summary: Utility transformer center-tap neutral bonded to ground at the service disconnect (main bonding jumper); downstream branch circuits use a separate EGC — functionally TN-S downstream of the bond.
decisions.rule: Grounding electrode + main bonding jumper per NEC 250.24

decisions.label: PSCC 10 kA → all breakers ≥ 10 kA AIC
decisions.summary: Declared 10 kA at MAIN-PANEL busbar; every branch breaker must have an interrupting rating ≥ 10 kA per NEC 110.9; UL 489 listing required.
decisions.rule: Interrupting rating ≥ available fault current

sections.title: Circuit Topology
sections.summary: All 8 branch circuits radial (US NEC has no ring-final-circuit construct; INV-04 satisfied trivially). Two kitchen small-appliance branches (C01/C02) per NEC 210.52(B); one general AFCI branch (C03) feeding living/bedrooms/hallway; five location-specific GFCI/AFCI branches (C04 bathroom, C05 garage, C06 outdoor, C07 basement, C08 laundry — laundry per 210.11(C)(2) requires a dedicated branch).

decisions.label: Radial-only topology (US jurisdiction)
decisions.summary: NEC 2023 does not provide a ring-final equivalent to BS 7671 §433.1.5. All branch circuits are radial-fed from the main panel via individual single-pole 120 V breakers.
decisions.rule: Branch-circuit configuration per NEC Article 210

decisions.label: Two kitchen small-appliance branches mandatory
decisions.summary: NEC 2023 Article 210.52(B)(1) requires at least two 20 A small-appliance branch circuits to serve countertop receptacles; this design provides exactly two (C01/C02) with 3 countertop receptacles each.
decisions.rule: Kitchen small-appliance branch-circuit requirement

decisions.label: Laundry on dedicated radial
decisions.summary: NEC 2023 Article 210.11(C)(2) requires an individual 20 A branch circuit for laundry receptacles with no other outlets. C08 satisfies this with a single 20 A GFCI breaker.
decisions.rule: Dedicated laundry branch circuit

sections.title: Special Locations
sections.summary: US NEC frames GFCI by location category in 210.8(A): bathrooms, garages, outdoors, basements, kitchens, laundry. AFCI per 210.12(A)/(B). Schema special_location enum is IEC-derived and doesn't encode US categories directly. Mapping: bathroom→bathroom_zone_3 (most permissive), outdoor→outdoor (native), and garage/basement/laundry→null with info-level flags documenting per-location NEC clauses.

decisions.label: Bathroom → bathroom_zone_3 schema mapping
decisions.summary: US NEC treats the entire bathroom as a GFCI zone (210.8(A)(1)) — there is no IEC-style zone 0/1/2/3 subdivision. The schema bathroom_zone_3 enum is the most permissive analogue; documented as info-flag.
decisions.rule: Bathroom GFCI per NEC; schema mapping by convention

decisions.label: Outdoor → outdoor (native enum)
decisions.summary: NEC 210.8(A)(3) outdoor receptacle GFCI requirement maps cleanly to the schema outdoor enum; in-use covers per NEC 406.9(B)(1) and weather-resistant receptacles per NEC 406.9(B)(2).
decisions.rule: Outdoor GFCI + weather-resistant + in-use cover

decisions.label: Garage / basement / laundry → null + info-flag
decisions.summary: Schema lacks enums for garage/basement/laundry. GFCI requirements still apply per NEC 210.8(A)(2)/(5)/(10) and are documented in non_compliance_flags as info-level; runtime gap noted for schema v1.1.
decisions.rule: NEC GFCI scope vs schema enum

sections.title: RCD Posture (US: GFCI + AFCI)
sections.summary: Schema rcd_posture is IEC-derived. US uses GFCI (4-6 mA per UL 943) and AFCI (UL 1699) — distinct from IEC RCDs. Pragmatic mapping: 7 GFCI / dual-function circuits (C01, C02, C04-C08) → type_a_30ma_per_§411_3_3 with rcd_type=GFCI + rcd_sensitivity_ma=10. C03 (AFCI only) → no_rcd_with_documented_§411_exception with citation. Schema gap flagged info-level for future v1.1.

decisions.label: AFCI+GFCI dual-function on kitchen branches (C01/C02)
decisions.summary: Kitchen countertop receptacles fall under both NEC 210.8(A)(6) (GFCI) and 210.12(B) (AFCI combination protection for kitchens/laundry as of NEC 2014+). Combination dual-function breakers per UL 489 + UL 943 + UL 1699 satisfy both.
decisions.rule: Combination AFCI+GFCI breakers for kitchen branches

decisions.label: AFCI-only on general dwelling branch (C03)
decisions.summary: Living rooms / bedrooms / hallway are outside NEC 210.8 GFCI scope but fall under NEC 210.12(A) AFCI protection (all 15/20 A 120 V branch circuits supplying receptacle outlets in dwelling units). AFCI breaker per UL 1699.
decisions.rule: AFCI protection for general dwelling branches

decisions.label: GFCI-only on location-specific branches (C04-C08)
decisions.summary: Bathroom (210.8(A)(1)), garage (210.8(A)(2)), outdoor (210.8(A)(3)), basement (210.8(A)(5)), laundry (210.8(A)(10)) — all require GFCI; AFCI not mandated for these locations in NEC 2023 (except 210.12(B) overlap for laundry — engineer specifies GFCI-only per scope; combination would be optional best-practice).
decisions.rule: Location-specific GFCI protection

decisions.label: Schema enum pragmatic mapping for GFCI
decisions.summary: GFCI (4-6 mA UL 943) maps to type_a_30ma_per_§411_3_3 + rcd_sensitivity_ma=10 (smallest enum) as the least-bad fit. Documented in compliance_summary as schema gap.
decisions.rule: Pragmatic schema mapping for US GFCI

sections.title: OCPD + Cable
sections.summary: C01/C02: 20 A AFCI+GFCI dual + 12 AWG NM-B. C03: 15 A AFCI + 14 AWG NM-B. C04-C08: 20 A GFCI + 12 AWG NM-B. All breakers 10 kA AIC (UL 489) matching the 10 kA PSCC. Curve C closest schema enum to US thermal-magnetic residential breakers (5-10× instantaneous). NM-B cable per NEC 2023 Article 334; PVC_70 encodes the 70 °C THHN/THWN equivalent.

decisions.label: 12 AWG NM-B for 20 A branches
decisions.summary: NEC 2023 Table 310.16 + 240.4(D)(5): 12 AWG copper has 25 A ampacity at 60 °C column, but the 240.4(D)(5) small-conductor limit caps 12 AWG at 20 A — exactly matching the branch breaker.
decisions.rule: Small-conductor 20 A limit on 12 AWG copper

decisions.label: 14 AWG NM-B for 15 A branch (C03)
decisions.summary: NEC 240.4(D)(3) limits 14 AWG copper to 15 A; C03 uses 14 AWG with a 15 A AFCI breaker.
decisions.rule: Small-conductor 15 A limit on 14 AWG copper

decisions.label: All breakers 10 kA AIC
decisions.summary: PSCC=10 kA at MAIN-PANEL → branch breakers AIC ≥ 10 kA per NEC 110.9; UL 489-listed product range covers AFCI / GFCI / dual-function 10 kA devices.
decisions.rule: Interrupting rating ≥ available fault current

sections.title: Diversity + Zs
sections.summary: Diversity per NEC 2023 Article 220.40-220.55. Small-appliance per 220.52(A) (1500 VA × 2 = 3000 VA); laundry per 220.52(B) (1500 VA); general-receptacle (C03) into 3 VA/ft² per 220.42, diversified per 220.42(A) (first 3000 VA @ 100%, balance @ 35%). Zs verification deferred to calc.zs_loop_impedance per WI3; all circuits flagged TOOL-CALL-PENDING.

decisions.label: Kitchen diversified to 12 A + 10 A on two branches
decisions.summary: NEC 220.52(A) sets the minimum at 1500 VA per branch (12.5 A at 120 V); engineer specifies 12 A + 10 A as design-stage estimates with the 20 A breakers giving comfortable headroom against simultaneous countertop appliance use.
decisions.rule: Kitchen small-appliance branch load per NEC 220.52(A)

decisions.label: Zs deferred to calc.zs_loop_impedance
decisions.summary: Ground-fault loop impedance Zs = Ze + (R1+R2) for verification of NEC 250.4(A)(5) effective-ground-fault-path requirement is computed by the calc skill; all 8 circuits flagged TOOL-CALL-PENDING. INV-08 satisfied.
decisions.rule: WI3 — deferred tool-call pattern

sections.title: Compliance + Assumptions
sections.summary: compliant=true. Four info flags: (a) schema special_location enum gap for US categories; (b) rcd_posture pragmatic mapping (US ≠ IEC §411); (c) NEC 406.12 tamper-resistant on all 31 receptacles; (d) Zs verification deferred per WI3. Nine assumptions; utility-declared Ze=0.25 Ω and PSCC=10 kA to be verified at first energization per NEC 110.24 field-marking.

decisions.label: All receptacles tamper-resistant
decisions.summary: NEC 2023 Article 406.12 requires all 15 A and 20 A 125 V receptacles in dwelling units to be listed tamper-resistant. All 31 receptacles in this design encode tamper_resistant in the socket type string.
decisions.rule: Tamper-resistant receptacles in dwelling units

decisions.label: Ze and PSCC declared, not measured
decisions.summary: Utility-declared values used for design; on-site verification at first energization per NEC 110.24 (field marking) and 250.66 (grounding electrode conductor sizing).
decisions.rule: Initial verification + fault-current field marking

sections.title: Drafting References
sections.summary: Single Arch_D sheet (24"×36") at 1/4"=1' imperial scale per AIA CAD Layer Guidelines 2007 (US AEC convention). Layer naming Discipline-MajorGroup-MinorGroup (e.g., E-POWR-RECP for receptacles, E-POWR-CIRC for circuits, E-POWR-PANL for panelboards). 1700 ft² single-story plate fits on Arch_D with panel schedule, GFCI/AFCI legend, and notes.

decisions.label: Arch_D @ 1/4"=1' single-sheet output
decisions.summary: US standard residential drawing sheet is Arch_D (24"×36") at quarter-inch scale; one sheet fits the entire single-story dwelling with panel schedule and notes.
decisions.rule: AIA CAD Layer Guidelines 2007 drawing conventions
